summary: Elderly patients rarely call 911 for suspected strokes because many episodes are fleeting or mild—such as transient ischemic attacks or syncopal events—that lack clear urgency or recognizable features prompting help-seeking behavior. The absence of consistent symptom severity limits their likelihood of seeking timely medical attention despite presenting clinically similar patterns to true stroke.
